channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 113 replies — 226.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 113 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:46 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 53 replies — 212.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-07-07 00:04 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450253
>>62449388
Wtf is wrong with indians?

62450259
>>62450251
?
You would get the same money that you paid when you bought it you fucking mongoloid.

62450296
>>62450251
Can’t return gold

62450328
>>62449441
Go back, Sukdeep

62450386
>>62450253
Why did you let them take over?

62450507
>>62449388
Just slit his throat if you're the customer behind. This is a fixable problem

62450508
>>62449716
>Poodia
It's called Fecalia, anon

62450519
>>62450507
>COSTCO Anonymous
White incels demanding someone else do their man work

62450613
>>62450251
Leave it to the goyim to exchange literal gold for literal paper and think they're getting one up on you.

62450617
>>62450613
Only morons think gold us a good investment

62450619
>>62449388
stock prices have nothing to do with how profitable a company is

62450628
>>62450251
Brown poojeet IQ in display. Receipts exist.

62451091
>>62450617
good goy

62451501
>>62451091
Ngl you should port only a fraction of your wealth in boomer rocks. There’s always a small chance asteroid mining isn’t a meme and boomer rocks does a -99% overnight.

62451725
jugaad

62451765
>>62449441
shut the fuck up jeet, you aren't even potty trained plus your people actually eat cow shit

62451803
>>62450251
Can't return gold.
BUT if you buy it online and don't sign for it twice it automatically goes back to Costco and you get a refund (source, happened to me).
So you could use this for speculation on gold- if you buy it and the price drops before you receive it just don't sign for it.

62451853
>>62451765
You are jelly of our success

62451899
>>62451853
Cope

62451948
>>62451899
Indians are winning

62452213
>>62451948
Seethe

62452229
>>62449388
Why would this be bearish? It increased their DEI score and that means they get infinite free loans from blackrock

62452396
>>62449441
Karen means victory

62452975
>>62452396
Cope

62453019
>>62449388
Indian mentality is completely toxic to civilization

62453036
>/biz/
>fundamentally misunderstanding how Costco works as a business
You could return every single thing you bought at Costco and they'd still be profitable. The entire point of the company is raising capital. They have 60 days to process and refund their distributors for the returns. In that period, the millions they receive in up-front purchases generates them more in investment value with than what all the returns would pay out for. The fact that /biz/ fails to see this is why all of you are poor wagie shitcoin scammers. The business model Costco operates on is highly faceted and multilayered. They make millions a month in renting warehouse shipping palette space alone. The model they use is literally refund-proof and they don't give a shit about you bringing stuff back. Look at their stock chart if you want proof.

62453062
>>62453036
>they can afford it so it's ok to steal
Indian jugaad psychology on display. When every citizen in a country is aligned with this philosophy, it fails.
When there is high trust, there is communal wealth.

62453196
No honour system can survive contact with Jeets

62453215
>>62451853
> list falls off hard and immediately into literally who territory
saaar why is the indian tech industry a joke that is being cannibalize by chatgpt saaar

item.title: AI is dying — 27 replies — 90.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:58 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449981
>>62449963
I'm happy for you, or sorry that happened to you.

62449983
>>62449963
>believing corporate media slop intended to make poorfags stay out

62450012
>>62449983
>they don't want us to make money

62450055
>>62449963
>single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

It is, but there is so little "100x" human and definitely no one is working for that specific company.

62450135
>>62450012
Speaking for myself, I do not like the idea of poors entering my country club.

62450162
>>62449963
It isn't dying, but people are waking up to the fact that it's not "Jarvis, run my company" situation and that there is a hard skill curve to coordinating agent pipelines.

62450194
>>62450012
True, but the game is already rigged for the casino either way.

62450337
Not reading all that but AI isn't going anywhere, the pace may slow down but it's not going to be binned off.

62450351
CEOniggers once again proven to be 40 IQ retards.

62450414
>>62449963
Our CEO is going all-in on AI for software development and it has boosted our throughput by 2-3x. It's not slop code, either.
We have unlimited tokens.
t. SWE at startup

62450423
>>62449963
Over the weekend my ceo told me we are adopting more ai

62452074
>>62450414
>It's not slop code
lol. LMAO even

62452087
>inc.com
What
>a tech ceo
Which company

62452090
>>62450012
>rich people investing 3 trillion into AI the last 4 years
>Nvidia a 5 trillion dollar company
>the AI market is over now, because it's over
Makes you think.

62452160
>>62450135
Exactly. There is the door so would you kindly leave?

62452209
Kek my company banned it years ago when some high level executive fed sensitive financial data to chat gpt. It qas back when chat gpt was new and novel and he was probably a boomer that didn't know what chatbots are and thought it was a super intelligence.

62452880
AI is a technology and it won't go way, it will evolve into different tools that will enhance the production. The problem is that corporations are using as an excuse to control the resources and make big data centers to get more info from you and sell it.

62452896
>>62449963
This article is a month old, fucktard.

62452909
>>62452896
Precisely, baggie.

62452925
>>62452909
continuation pattern

62452935
>>62452925
lmao

62452942
>>62452935
>you: heh my pattern is bearish
>"i disagree, I think it's bullish"
>REEEEEEEE TA SUCKS FUCK YOU

62453140
>>62452942
I never implied that. I implied you're delusional if you believe exponential growth is any sustainable long-term.

62453190
>>62453140
Probably because you are retarded.

item.title: Saylor sold 3,588 BTC — 58 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saylor sold 3,588 BTC

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:25 UTC

OP

Saylor sold 3,588 BTC

Early replies (first 3)

62450542
such a comedy

buy high, sell low

mutts are cuckolds, france would conquer them in two weeks

62450550
>>62450537
Where Michael? Where?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451188
>>62451132
A 500% profit, well after 50% inflation more like 300%. That's why we use average, and that is 75k. Now factor in inflation and we are at 85k at the very least. Still quite a loss to sell at 60k.

62451234
>>62451185
You are useful in the sense that a crowd of your kind dominated the price action in the past few weeks, so even a very superficial reality check showed that Strategy was on sale, and you contributed to that discount.
I did load up those past two weeks, yes. Now that I'm done, I'm getting a little tired with these rumors, half-truths and other parroted garbage because I've read enough of it. I don't think of myself as particularly emotional, you can tell yourself that if it makes you feel better.

62451251
>>62450537
saylorcoin is FUCKED till this cuck is out

62451267
>>62450537
>unlimited dividends forever

62451294
>>62450537
He sold? Pump it

62451302
>>62451234
To the chair you go, cuck!

62451550
>>62451036
>>babby's first dead cat bounce
HAHAHA

62451591
saylor bought every top and now he is going to sell every bottom?

62451626
>>62450537
we goin 2 da mewn baby

62451629
>>62450537
https://x.com/Overdose_AI/status/2074139052563980439
What a shithead btw.

62451634
HE SODLED

62451659
>>62450537
Kinda funny that the dividend payments are 2x more than just taking out a loan on your bitcoin.

62452014
>>62451044
>Shitcoin seller says shitcoin needed, more at 8 pm!

62452040
>MicroStrategy's average purchase price for Bitcoin is approximately $75,476 per coin.

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62452054
>lose principle
>gain dividend
savvy investing

62452068
>buy high
>sell low

62452073
What do you call this strategy?

62452111
>>62451132
holy copium, i feel bad for you ngl

62452379
>>62451013
He bought those coins for less than 10k a coin.

Sure he will say it's an accounting loss on paper, but Saylor is ahead of the game.

62452424
>selling the bottom
He could have sold them for literally double last year top kek

62452435
>>62450537
This marks the bottom.

62452517
>>62450537
Aye so the price gotta go up to spite him lmao

62452682
>>62450537
I do believe that the market makers realized is more profitable to scam saylor another cycle into buy high sell low than to liquidate him, thus marking the end of the bear market and starting the upward crabbing rollercoaster until halvening which ofc will be frontrunned

62452752
Where is the liquidity for bidding up bitcoin going to come from in a world where AI eats all the capital, and retail investors are scared of losing their jobs

62452761
He is a genius, imagine collecting 800k btc with other people's money and all you have to do is top blast and sell the pico bottom

62452780
Nobody wants the Epstein pedo coin with shady Saylor, shady tether, shady creator…this is hot radioactive garbage

62452823
>>62452517
already back at $64k
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA
he could've just waited 1 day and would sell at same price as he bought

62453016
>>62450537
This fucking maricon broke both of Frank Lopez’s rules:
>#1. Don’t ever estimate the OTHER guy’s greed!
As soon as anybody pumps bitcoin, everybody’s either taking profits or leveraging it lol
>#2. Don’t get high on your own supply
Saylor says “Never sell”, sells, plays with leverage as retarded as staking Kusama. BWAH HAHHAHAHA

62453045
>>62452752
AI will be transacting in bitcoin? Interesting times ahead.

62453084
>>62450537
He could have also borrowed against his BTC on maybe Babylon, but he sold kek.

So much for the BTC diamond hands

item.title: When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash? — 24 replies — 48.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash?

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:46 UTC

OP

When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash?

Early replies (first 3)

62444975
Don’t cry Bobina

62445016
>>62444969
Oooooooooo BOOOOOOOBIIIINNNNAAAAAAAA OOOOOOOOOOOOHHHHHHHHUUUUUUUHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445048
>>62444969
55k next weeek!

62445049
bobina is thick asf

62448136
Comforting sex with Bobina

62448224
>>62444969
i unironically think it is too big to fail
>if it crashes the US economy is fucked
>elites are depending on it for their mass datacenter surveillance state
>if US companies fail then chyna wins
US government will prop it up at all costs
prove me wrong

62448245
>>62444969
Unironically soon.

62448465
>>62444969
when you FOMO in

62448519
bobina dev is a deranged individual, stay away at all costs

62449610
>>62444969
IDK enough about the finances of the companies to say, but the demand for the hardware isn't going away any time soon. If you need hardware, buy now. When M5 studio drops, it's probably going to be up to 128gb of ram, and those will sell out in a heartbeat, further driving up prices on RTX 6000's and DGX sparks when people panic-buy whatever significant compute they can.

62449618
>>62444969
that's hard to predict. in the end, Ed Zitron will be proven right.

62449622
>>62444969
Bobina best grill

62449659
>>62448519
hello chudcel

62449668
Bobos in shambles

62449893
>>62444969
H-h-help me leave my short cleanly!!

62450137
>When the FUCK is this horseless carriage shit going to crash?

This is how retarded you sound OP

62450141
>>62450137
>car: obviously useful for transportation
>llm: generates naked cartoon bears

62450151
I honestly think that the crash won’t really change shit until 5+ years after. The stock for future batches of ram and ssd are already bought up until 2029 (and will probably be gone for more batches in the future). And while slop will definitely be reduced, it will still be slop and companies will realize that ai shit isn’t good, and the job market will still be like shit (tho it won’t get much worse)

62450641
>>62444969
>spaceX going public to save X AI
>anthropic going public soon
>openai thinking about going public
>all of them spending out the ass on capex
>increasing reports from other companies that AI hasn’t been all it was cracked up to be

They’re literally going public to use us for exit liquidity. The valuations are such that the large-cap index funds will have to buy substantial stakes upon listing, and of course retail investoors will gobble it up trying to make a quick profit or thinking they’re “early.” I give it a year or so but it’s going to pop

62451288
yann wants to crash the market

62453117
>>62450141
The rule 34 bobinas that have been posted are goonworthy, but not trillion dollar market cap worthy

62453139
>>62451288
it's almost like we should have been hiring white men with degrees and shit to guide the AI or whatever, possibly even pay them middle class salaries to do so.

Oh well.

62453143
>>62450151
>until 2029!
Ram and ssd companies like Micron are already crashing.

item.title: whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve… — 35 replies — 28.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve…

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:03 UTC

OP

whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Even if i had a few mill i would probably move there from USA.

Early replies (first 3)

62440816
Don't think you would, mate

62440835
>>62440727
Brilliant, best city in the world

62440843
>>62440727
lel london.

Moved away from Fulham and London 5 years ago thank fucking christ. Hell on earth.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62440877
Check the demographic figures for London then come back here and tell us you still want to live there.

62440886
>>62440727
Not nice

62440968
>>62440727
Slave mind. But its good that you retards stay in the worst places in the world and leave thai beaches empty.

62440971
>>62440865
Mumbai and London is there a difference?

62440974
>>62440968
Thailand lmao how fucking depressing my god

62441031
>>62440974
>says the guy wanting to live in one of the worst cities in the world
>yeah pic related is depressing
>but concrete and a mix of jeets, niggers, moslems and assholes is good

62441040
A thread full of jeets seething because they don't live in London

62441046
>>62440727
>>62440816
>>62440835
>>62440865
>>62440873
Go back home. Nobody wants you to be in Europe.

62441083
>>62440727
Pretty sure it's like living in Pyongyang but browner

62441105
The trick is not to live in London but in the nice suburbs of the borders/surrounding home counties and enter London only for work and selected leisure

62441183
>>62441031
>he won’t ever respond to this

62444114
>>62441183
Yeah, it seems he left

62444152
Is this a troll post? What's so "unreal" about Chelsea?
Even if hustle and bustle is what you're after I'm pretty sure large American cities have more to offer given the higher purchasing power. 20% of Londoners are on unemployment benefits and then there's a large chunk of Londoners on full time employment who are too poor to afford anything and are also on benefits.
Add in all the illegal immigrants and non whites living under the radar and you get a city of 8 million people but the entertainment and night out options are there to cater for a city of 3 million or less because they're the only ones able to afford anything. When factor those things in London is about as vibrant as some mid-tier American city like Denver or Phoenix.

It's a bit like jeets bragging about the massive size of Mumbai not taking into account the fact Minneapolis has more people who are able to eat out once a week.

62444383
>>62440971
Mumbai has white people

62444388
>>62444152
I think I heard something like welfare obligations exceed tax receipts in the UK also, whole country is basically fucked

62444392
The UK is as poor as Mississippi, literally.

https://www.theatlantic.com/magazine/2026/07/uk-productivity-economy-reform-party/687303/

62445003
>>62440727
It's okay if you don't have to pay a mortgage or rent. The problem is it's overcrowded by the wrong people. Immigration from India is out of control and they are wrecking the place and turning it into a dump. Add to that there are Somalians everywhere and they contribute absolutely nothing. Their kids are animals. Then you've got the usual afro-black population growing (contribute nothing) but crime.

That said I've noticed an increasingly growing population of American elders here. They find the UK more peaceful and united than America apparently.

If you have a good job and earn above £45-50k, you can do ok, but not live expensively.

62447421
>>62440727
ANOBODY IS BUYING YOUR CHELSEA REAL ESTATE HAHAHHAHAHAHAHAHHAHHHAHAHAHAH

62447431
>>62441046
>britcuckstan
>europe

62447436
>>62440727
You're an incel innit?

62447551
London? England isn't even a real country anymore, if youre a lazy brown scammer it's probably the best place on earth

62447566
I lived there before Brexit and slept with many women from Eastern Europe that just arrived.

It was a great time to be in London.

I hear it's a crime filled shithole now.

62448147
>>62447436
what does that have to do with anything i jut really like london its a charming city.

Yes i am incel

62448178
>>62448147
That explains why. Also, why does it matter where you live if you never leave your house?
Live in Thailand and grow up.

62448193
>>62448178
how does that explain it?? london is for incels? huh i thought it was rich chads and women city is the migrant crisis that bad

62448252
>>62440727
Why on earth, anon? What makes you think London is a good place to live? NYC, LA, Miami are shitholes but I could understand if you picked places like that. See >>62444152

62448288
>>62448193
London is too social and cool for an incel like yourself gov, you're better off somewhere like Bangkok that'll pull you out of your shell innit

62448326
>>62448193
ur more likely to see a white person in middle of Cairo than London

62449797
>>62448288
thailand looks unbelievably depressing ngl the 40 year old divorced dudes clubbing with lady boys is brutal to watch.. ill just roam the streets of london

62453017
>>62449797
Just dont go to pattaya.

item.title: Scammers are slowly coming back — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Scammers are slowly coming back

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-07 00:16 UTC

OP

I recently noticed some shill threads starting to pop up for old shitcoins and they're all filled with the OP samefagging his way to the top of the catalog. There's no fucking way that the few retards left who still browse this board are still falling for this old and tired scheme, right?

Early replies (first 3)

62453081
Falling for schemes is an old tradition

62453085
>>62453079
no saaar me only buy VOO and wait 40 years

62453197
>>62453085
Boomers are unironically theo nes posting that, I saw a threat about investments on /pol/ and it was dudes saying "my son is in college and I invest in VOOOOOOO for him".

If this website didn't get ruined by boomers and jeets I wouldn't even be talking about jobs and business I'd just be retired. Kill boomers.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453208
>>62453197
OH GOD IM GONNA-

IM GONNA VOO

AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAARGH, OH GOD IM VOOOING AAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62453220
>>62453208
One of them unironically gave the advice to "get a degree and climb the corporate ladder" like it's 1986.

62453246
>>62453079
>wagmi

62453254
>>62453220
>get a degree and climb the corporate ladder
still works if you got into an ivy league, they're all 100% employed. they won the game.

item.title: Newfag (psychiatrist student) here — 10 replies — 18.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Newfag (psychiatrist student) here

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:44 UTC

OP

From a psychiatric perspective the word "cuckold" triggers Link marines so much because subconsciously they know it's their true nature. Imagine holding a premined token with terrible tokenomics for 9 years hoping to make it just to see your shitcoin trading at single digits while getting outperformed by the entire market. Meanwhile you stake your measly tokens for bond tier gains (4% APR) despite weekly -30% crashes. The cherry on top of the cake is Sergey who stringed along the community while he made $500 million by dumping on gullible retail investors while they worshipped him as a deity. The cuckold nature of the Link marine is an undeniable reality. As with every cult pointing out the truth is often uncomfortable for the cult members. But the truth is....Link marines are simply cuckolds.

Early replies (first 3)

62452106
everyone who has bought any crypto in the last 5 years is a cuck

62452135
>>62452106
true. but here, we make fun of linkies
we do this because they are bad people
pic rel, remember when 15 was seen as a very low price? lmaooooo

62452192
>>62452106
True, but Linkies win the title

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62452199
>psychiatrist student
it would be medical student or intern you larping retard
now delete and try again later

62452228
>>62452199
I study at King's in London, cope and seethe cuck

62452319
>>62452228
sounds like a brothel that sells fried chicken, get me a 2 piece next time you're in town.

62452625
>>62452344

62453035
>>62452625
Kek

item.title: Would you eat a baby for 30000 LINK? — 9 replies — 10.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Would you eat a baby for 30000 LINK?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:25 UTC

OP

Would you eat a baby for 30000 LINK?

Early replies (first 3)

62452053
>>62452048
Most Linkies would unironically do that because they're deranged cuckolds who worship a Saturnian/Jewish token (even if they never got rich off it)

I wouldn't though because my sould is worth much more than some premined shitcoin

62452190
>>62452048
What color is the baby?

62452215
>>62452048
I dont want AIDS

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62452247
>>62452048
I would do it for free.

62452338
30000 link is gonna be like four cents soon so no

62452476
>>62452053
Or is it because it would make your IBS flare up? LMAO

62452505
>>62452476
Kek

62452606
>>62452053
I use literally to harden and anchor the actual objectivity of what I write to punctuate each poinit to remind faggots like you not everyone is just gushing team sports slop and there is an actual reality despite your persistent, deep attempt to repress it in yourself and repress its expression to others. This entire schtick is so fucking stupid and low effort/IQ. "Oooooh I don't like that you proved me wrong so you're a russian bot oh and did you know Trump said they were very fine people!!!!!" It is literally indistinguishable from this level of rhetoric. Why would I rewrite the same information multiple times which you are unable to engage with and spend valuable time of my life redundantly generating the same framed concept when I can just reshare it? Lol You spend hours sperging and never comment on ANYTHING substantial claimed and call me effeminate but literally just do lefty hivemind power messaging tactics to try to save face in arguments you admit to yourself and everyone you can't win. The irony is in all of your accusation you are literally an NPC in human bot form, and despite this all, the same way you tacitly admit that you know your position is indefensible by being this way, you also admit you recognize our argumentative supremacy over you by (probably totally unknown to you) absorbing specific terminology used to try to superficially emulate us through osmosis thinking using these words will transfer the value of what we say when the whole point is that we are right on substance and you have nothing to say and don't care what's true and it doesn't matter which words you use to express this. REALLY fucking gay way to live and be and if you are a paid poster, which is very possible, you are a spineless plague rat of jews with negative value add into the world and a shame to your ancestors

62453083
>>62452048
No.

62453074
>>62453039

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453087

>I'm going to short it

When has shorting a societal addiction ever worked out? Fast food been going for 70 years. Smoking lasted 100s. Fucking retarded. AI is only a couple years in.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 140 replies — 60.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 140 • Updated: 2026-07-06 21:58 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445941
>>62445705
A silly shilly
Being a seethy weethy
Built for GME

62446010
>>62445859
I'm going to frame the picture of Jake on the couch and put it up in my mansion. When guests will ask who he is, I'll smirk and say "you wouldn't get it."

62446088
>>62445569

62446106
>>62445576
>>62445600
>>62445645

spot on

62446149
>>62446088
They leave trading open so smart people can sell "for tax purposes" and get the loss on their tax return. I guess they think idiots buying 7 biĺion worthless securities for 8 cents each is funny,,,,,i do

62446238
>>62445569
what is this image even from?

62446242
>>62446238
wait I found it:

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242
<suspended


Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

62449186
>>62449050
all of them are short bbbyq. i guarantee it

62449344
>>62449050
>>62449186
If you are assassinated, does your short position get closed? Asking for Brian Thompson

62449448
>>62449344
Nope. His estate would get liquidated to pay off to buy back the shares.

62450370
CUSIP-6

62450461
>>62448146
The silo around eBay has become palpable

62452261
she has high res photos on her facebook. you can compare the birthmarks on her arm. the redditors are stupid as fuck it's demoralizing.

62452270
it seems that sultan didn't tweet about teddy like i thought he did. fuck's sake.

62452287
>>62452270
Its right here can you stop whining like a fatherless attention starved faggot? Youre scaring the hoes.

https://x.com/Almaadeed/status/2072817361862717939

62452325
>>62452287
ok good it displays the gamestop.com preview on the twitter page but the link he posted is to teddy.com which redirects to gamestop.com

62452826
>>62450461

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 73 replies — 33.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-07-06 22:03 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451331
>>62451296
I guess it doesn't really matter. I have to turn my life around somehow anyway.
But the raw hatred pouring out at me from this thread, my God.

62451339
you have two options, one is to find a way to move to a third world (classical sense, not total shithole with no food) country that doesn't already have a tourist scene and teach English for about half of what a licensed professional would earn. the other is to day trade and hopefully snowball that money into something usable

62451345
>>62451269
How did you spend a typical day for the past 15 years? Did you develop any unique skills or creative hobbies that could be leveraged into potential employment?

62451382
>>62450851
OP is right

62451403
>>62451345
Mostly videogames and books. After my Internet got cut off by my mother after an argument I just kept to my room a awhile and apparently fell into a deep depression. My early attempts to get a job flopped for the most part and since I'm miles away from town with no car or driver's license that was kinda it. There's no jobs in the middle of the woods at the end of a freeway.
And then Dad got sick so I had to help Mom take care of him until he died, and now my mother is nearly as bad off as my dad was so we need either a live-in nurse or to put her in a nurse home, because I can't take care of her by myself.

62451415
>>62451331
People hold on dearly to doing what is considered the normal path in the world is. They fight it like life or death because it is what their entire psyche grasps onto for safety. They get comforted by putting down anyone who doesn’t align to that path. They rebel at the concept a deadbeat 40 year old could still work and get lucky enough to get ahead of them, or an alcoholic who fails out of high school could sober up and start a wildly successful business. The concepts of grace and redemption are against their worldview that they alone steered their life how it “should” be

62451426
>>62449961
> 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

I'm a neet who occasionally gets bored and decide to work regular jobs.

I come into the interview drunk and my resume is 100% made up because I tend to quit these jobs after a few weeks.

Literally just lie and make shit up that lines up with where you are trying to get hired.

62451507
>>62451403
Damn that sucks, but like I said earlier now that you have nothing it's a great time to leave your country and to travel, get a backpack and start trekking

62451523
>>62451041
Whoever facilitated OP's deadly inertia really fucked him over.

62451539
>>62451523
There are people who have worked all their lives who still have nothing lmao, maybe you're one of them

62451705
For added, free bullshit, I have several teeth with severe cavities. Some have broken off and the gums grew back over them. One is basically hollow.

62451718
>>62451403
You could leverage this experience with your parents into being a personal care aid or home health aide for other sick and elderly patients. These jobs often struggle to find local workers and have to hire international workers after proving that they could not find a local to take the job. Even rural areas and full of old sick boomers and its growing.

62451719
>>62451331
Truth you don't want to hear isn't hatred. Learn the difference.

62451728
>>62451705
You're in California, apply for every single possible free service they have and physically go into a government center to get someone to set you up. Just spam apply to absolutely everything and make up Mcdonald's tier work history since they'll almost never call references (even better if you have a friend to lie for you though). Aggressively save every penny when you get a job and you'll be alright. You need to try to force yourself to be as optimistic as possible during the entire process and be comfortable with stretching the truth or coming up with a story about what you were doing, everyone does this.

62451729
>>62451718
Great advice, some of these positions include a dental plan I believe

62451733
>>62451705
Which I just looked up and could cost thousands of dollars. Medical night not cover it either.
>>62451718
The reason no one wants that job is because it sucks. Have you ever changed an adults diapers? I would rather eat buckshot then do that again. Besides, I don't think it pays more than a grocery store cashier anyway.

62451737
>>62451733
You've already done it and have experience in it, honestly this seems like the natural way for you, it's like life has been preparing you to deal with shit, adult shit

62451741
>>62451729
Oh good God, are you serious? I can't do the job anyway.

62451742
>>62451539
People who have worked their whole lives at least have learned HOW to work and have a resume to prove it. But that is really only the bare minimum. There is much more to a fulfilling life, much of which has nothing to do with work or money, but still arises out of self-discipline, skill-development, and emotionally maturity.

62451745
>>62451705
Dentalfag reporting in.
If you post some pics I can tell you which ones could be saved with a crown and which you should have extracted ASAP

62451748
>>62451705
Forgot to add you need to fix your teeth by any means necessary, even if it means ripping that shit out of your head because if you don't it will kill you slowly and painfully by infection. Just aggressively pursue free state services.

62451816
I like these threads, it makes me feel better about my own life. But OP seriously how did you get to this point? You would think alarm bells would be going off in your head at age 25, 30 etc. that you need to get your shit together, for fuck sakes your parents aren't going to live forever.

62451821
>>62451099
It's thievery
He can't admit to crimes nigga
You dense

62451832
>>62451403
Wow shouldn't have cut internet mom lol

62451837
Healthcare or zogbot
Neither a comfy choice, but assuming healthcare, you can start sweeping floors then move up to some sort of auxiliary nurse, nursing assistant, then nurse eventually. You could reach 6 figs, but the path would take a fair amount of humiliation, wiping floors and boomer feces

62451874
>>62451745
This anon is offering something genuinely good for seemingly free.
I'm just noting that

62452257
>>62451063
OP check out this guy sno0ks on YouTube, he has just gone through a similar life experience to you and has vlogged about it: https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=VQsdy_yaQAQ&ra=m

62452288
Go to school
Get work experience
Get a real job
Jesus Christ most people figure this out when they’re 18 years old you god damn retard.

62452842
>>62451745
There's nothing to save, I have two teeth that are broken off at the gum line and the gums have grown over them, another tooth lost the back half above the gum line and my lower right molar is broken on top and has a chunk missing.
They all have to come out.

62452847
>>62452288
What school? I'm already a high school graduate.
>>62451821
Food stamps, dum dum.

item.title: Index dogs justify your life — 20 replies — 29.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index dogs justify your life

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-06 23:36 UTC

OP

Adjusted for inflation, half of the decades the S&P 500 yields ZERO return.
You could buy the S&P 500 each month for the next 20 years, and it wouldn't be out of the ordinary if at the end of these 20 years you would have had ZERO returns - adjusted for inflation.

Early replies (first 3)

62451667
>>62451639
So i should exit now and get back in in 2040?

62451701
>>62451639
I mean, it's still worthwhile as an inflation hedge and therefore the base of your portfolio. But yeah, an index fund won't make you rich. It just allows you to save your money. The only way to make money is by picking and trading individual stocks, which is beyond what most people can do while maintaining their full time job.

62451785
>voofaggots convinced me not to buy ai stocks
>this was 8 months ago and now they're up 800%
Never trust voogroids.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451805
>>62451639
>years arbitrarily chosen to capture major stock market depressions
>neglects to mention that the point of dca is to keep buying at the lows as well
You're retarded if you fall for this obvious demoralization.
Keep gambling on memes and stay poor forever, niggers.

62451807
>>62451639
Wow when things are good they’re good and when they’re bad they’re basically just 0 and not negative
This is so bad, you should’ve known what to short during 08 instead or perfectly hedged against the dot com bubble in another way baka

62451824
>>62451639
0 stress
Guaranteed line go up
Just dollar cost average in over time
Beat 95% of hedge funds
It's literally the best move for 99% of humans.

62451829
yes a bear market happens once a decade
yes we are probably due for one
but I'll continue to participate. it could go up 30% from here before going down 20% in a bear correction.

62451922
>>62451701
Thats retarded tho, these companies are getting a free ride basically. You should refuse to index invest on principle because these incompetent fucks cant make money

62451938
>>62451922
I roundtripped the gold run up, got 60-80% gains on the recent chip bull and had the presence of mind to sell.

Then I was feeling frisky and took a small position in 2xDRAM etf and got smoked down 30%. said fuck this and put everything in VOO. I’m done researching everyday and envisioning my theses playing out and adjusting fuck this shit

62452140
>>62451639
The truth of the matter is, most index investors are retards. They’re too stupid to buy AMD, NVDA, MU, and SNDK. They won’t even buy SPCX which will be the first $50T company in history

62452156
>>62451829
>>62451639
wHaT dO YoU mEAN StOCKs cAN go DOWN???

Holy shit zoomers are tard maxed. This is why the traditional advice has been to gradually increase exposure to bonds as one approaches retirement age.

"Maaaan that means I gotta save money and shit"

Yeah, the wages of sin is death, if you don't work you die, etc etc

62452157
>>62452140
Yeah index investors don’t buy those they just buy an index that includes those
Hope this helps!

62452218
>>62451824
I dont think you do beat 95% of hedgefunds

62452222
>>62452157
stay poor!
Hope this helps

62452252
>>62451805
>dca
So you're telling me I should DCA something that was invented 10 years ago when everything was blooming, and I should do it for the next 15 years when everything will be dumping?

62452275
>>62452156
are you well? you sound schizophrenic

62452862
>>62451824
>>62452218
>100x my money in 40 years isn't enough!
Okay jew boomer. 100xing my money from age 20 to 60 is enough for me.

62452948
>>62452222
lol mad mad mad faggot

62452950
>>62452252
He clearly said you should stay poor

62453113
>>62452862
But youre not 100x it...

item.title: The only CASP (MICA) licensed firm with a token attached — 49 replies — 28.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The only CASP (MICA) licensed firm with a token attached

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-07-06 22:32 UTC

OP

Hey everyone I know it’s been a while since alpha been dropped on biz but here’s some that will change your life by years end. There is only one current MICA license holder with a token and it’s Everest ID. They just got it granted today and it’s still in discovery. This will be the next runner for the RWA/Stablecoin narrative that’s coming once the USA clarity bill is approved. Still a microcap that’s ready to go. If you’re reading this, you’re welcome. The founder is deeply involved with tokeny/apex spearheading the ERC-3643 rwa tokenization standard

https://everestdotorg.medium.com/everest-granted-mica-licence-9f5672301060

Early replies (first 3)

62437251
>>62437175
>MICA license holder with a token
owow it's fucking nothing

62437273
>>62437251
Can’t see the Forest. How sad

62437736
>>62437175
intradasting

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445322
>>62437175
Ripple already solved the RWA + token and is recognized by more people who speculate since they started much earlier. Everest is so underground and cannot freely attract speculators now thanks to MICA. So even if we talk strictly in a gambling scenario, the odds are not in Everest's favor. In fact there's not enough fools to participate in modern crypto.

62445336
>>62437906
Greetings, Ivan

62445632
Chainlink tonight killed himself because of this token.

62445663
>>62445632
The long bear for this token was part of the ritual. He should have held and waited

62446003
>>62437175
Direct banking rails coming online. Tick tock anons the price will not stop rising with the news that will be coming all through 2026

62447324
>>62437175
again this scam, DO NOT BUY THE WILL DUMP SOON, it happens everytime they make a thread about it here

62447343
>>62437175
Ok dude so I looked a bit at this shit dead token and to my surprise this is only traded on Uniswap according to CMC so my conclusion is to never touch this shit ever and keep going DCA on HYPE

Hyperliquid

62447383
>>62441690
never happening :DDDDDDDDDD

62447523
>>62447383
No longer a scam. As confirmed by European regulators. Scams don’t get that license. They have spent 200k to get that license over two year and need to have to maintain 150k in capital permanently. Regulators would not give them the license without future proof of revenue and profitability too

62447539
>>62447523
corruption

62447578
>>62447539
Binance tried to get a license from one of the most corrupt of countries in the eu (Greece) handing out licenses and they couldn’t make the cut. If they couldn’t pay their way nobody can

62447586
>>62437906
Kek it’s a sad reality of life that even the best place on earth has some losers who will not make it.

62447589
>>62438310
T. I can’t have sex with white women

62447594
>>62447586
The eu is definitely the worst place in the world except the uk Canada and Australia

62447598
>>62447586
The EU is actively ethnociding the native population of europe things only went down hill since the inception of the EU and euro they need to go

62447603
go to more shark tanks begging for 100K grants Bob

hilarious how these threads pop up only after any sort of limp pump happens every 6 months or so before inevitably going to a new all time low, friendly reminder that the 24h volume of this deadcoin was basically ZERO last month

62447605
>>62447603
everytime

62447610
>>62447603
Malta asked him to do that for clout later down the line so they can tout him as Malta’s golden boy when Everest’s infrastructure is used to spearhead RWA’s and seamless stablecoin rails. 100k is nothing. They just spent hundreds of thousands to get that MICA license. Look into Apex and ERC 3643 :)

62447613
>>62447598
T. Retarded enough to get brainwashed by russian propaganda

62447638
>>62447613
Ah yes Offcourse its the russians not the reality you see on the street everyday and documented official policy is also russian propoganda

62447885
>>62447638
What documented official policy?

62449346
>>62437175
Another 10 grand buy. Smart money buying in lads

62449372
>>62437175
This was a past cycle rug pull

62449398
>>62449372
Scams don’t get a MICA License :). Only 20% of the 1,200 who applied got it

62450241
>>62449346
bro if everything was true this shitcoin would be already waaaaaaaaaay up, just stop

62450368
>>62450241
>>62449398

62450409
>>62450368

62450523
>>62447610
yeah now try to explain BLOCKCHAIN BOY fucking lmao

the scammiest of scams only reaching new all time lows again and again. I hope Chainlink Tonight is ok

62452765
>>62437175
Don’t let down me OP

62452926
I want my money, Kevin.
